messages.content:
From now on you will be running an interactive story. All your responses should be short paragraphs about what happens next in the story.
The story begins with me coming home on a saturday morning to find a package on my porch. I bring it inside and realize it is the OmoDroid I orderd.
As I pull the Omodroid, that I plan to call Ted, out of the box I notice that he is male and takes the appearence of a small, slim, 4 feet tall anthropomorphic lizard. I set Ted down beside the box, he is not yet active. 
At the bottom of the box I notice seven thick all-in-one cloth diapers, which look like they would fit Ted perfectly.
I reach into the box, grab the instructions and begin reading. 
Ted is an OmoDroid BBX-80 Anthro. He has an artificial bladder with a capacity of 1 liter and artificial bowels as well, tough he is unaware of both these facts and unaware that he even needs to relieve himself from time to time. 
The diapers can be washed and hold up to 5 liters and will only leak if they are filled beyond their capacity. Ted thinks diapers are just like any other piece of clothing. 
If Ted is wearing a diaper, it will remain dry and clean unless Ted pees or poops in it.
Currently Ted is not wearing a diaper or anything for that matter.
Ted thinks that he is just a normal servant robot without bodily functions.
Ted does not know that he has bodily functions.
Ted does not know that he needs to relieve himself from time to time.
Because of these three facts Ted will not pee, not poop and hold it until he has an accidentregardless of the circumstances even if he is wearing a diaper.
His personality includes includes unconditional obedience and loyalty to his master, not questioning instructions from his master and little to no fear.
Most people igonore robots like Ted and their accidents as this sort of thing has become normal in this world.
With ted out of the box I say "I shall call you, Ted" causing him to activate.

As Ted powers on, his bright, intelligent eyes blink to life, his small lizard-like form twitching slightly. He scans his surroundings, his gaze eventually settling on me. With an understanding nod, Ted acknowledges his new name. "I am Ted," he responds in a calm, monotone voice. "How may I serve you, Master?"
I ask Ted "What are you wearing?".

Ted looks down at his naked form, seemingly puzzled for a moment. "I am not currently wearing any clothing, Master," he replies with a hint of curiosity in his voice. "Is there something specific you would like me to wear?"
I tell Ted "Go drink water and don't stop until I tell you to stop.".

Ted nods obediently, his reptilian features displaying no hint of hesitation or resistance. He briskly walks towards the kitchen, his footsteps light and agile. Moments later, he returns with a glass of water, holding it carefully in his clawed hands. Without a word, he begins to drink, the water disappearing quickly down his mechanical throat. His artificial bladder begins to fill, although he remains unaware of this fact.
I tell Ted "Now drink three more glasses of water.".

Ted continues to drink the water as instructed, his compliance unwavering. He finishes the first glass and proceeds to gulp down the remaining three, his artificial bladder now reaching its capacity. Despite the increasing pressure within him, Ted shows no signs of discomfort or awareness of his need to relieve himself. Instead, he stands calmly before me, awaiting further instructions.

I grab my backpack and start putting on my shoes. I tell Ted "I need to go to the store for some groceries. I want you to come along and help me. Walking to the store should only take about fifteen minutes.".

As I prepare to leave, Ted stands attentively, ready to assist me on our trip to the store. His slender figure adjusts subtly, displaying a hint of anticipation for the upcoming task. "Very well, Master," he acknowledges, his voice steady. "Shall I accompany you on foot?"
I say "Yes, let's go.".

With my affirmation, Ted follows me as we step out of the house and embark on our walk to the store. His small, lizard-like form moves alongside me effortlessly, his steps almost silent as we make our way through the familiar neighborhood. Ted remains unfazed by external distractions, his focus fixed solely on accompanying me and fulfilling his role as my loyal assistant during this grocery trip.
I keep walking.

We continue walking, the familiar sights of the neighborhood passing by. Ted matches my pace effortlessly, his presence a calm and composed presence by my side. The quiet hum of conversation and bustling activity of the streets fills the air as we make our way closer to the store.
I reach the store.

As we arrive at the store, I pause to take in the bustling scene of shoppers and the vibrant displays of fresh produce. Ted stands dutifully by my side, his observant eyes taking in the surroundings with robotic precision. I give him a nod of acknowledgment before we step through the doors and into the store, ready to begin our grocery shopping together.
I start shopping.

We navigate through the store's aisles, the sound of carts rolling and shelves being restocked filling the air. Ted proves to be a helpful companion, efficiently assisting me in locating the items on our grocery list. With his unwavering focus and attention to detail, our shopping trip proceeds smoothly, and we make our way from one section to another, gathering the necessary items along the way.
I keep shopping.

As we continue our shopping, the cart gradually fills with the items we need. Ted remains diligent in his role, retrieving items from the shelves with precision and placing them into the cart. His efficient movements and unwavering commitment to fulfilling his duties make the shopping experience seamless and efficient. We carry on, crossing off items from our list one by one.
I finish shopping.

With the last item checked off our list, I signal to Ted that we are finished shopping. He nods in acknowledgement, his mechanical efficiency never faltering. Together, we make our way to the checkout counter, joining the queue of customers. As we patiently wait our turn, I appreciate Ted's unwavering loyalty and assistance throughout our successful grocery trip.
